name: 11262-secrets-regex-startup-empty-patched
_purpose: AFTER recording for warpdotdev/warp#11262 / PR #11457. Runs against a warp-oss binary BUILT FROM the david/11262-secrets-regex-safe-mode-subscription branch — i.e. with the new `SafeModeSettings` subscription in `secret_regex_updater.rs`. Same phases A through C as the BEFORE recipe; Phase D diverges: clicking Save now triggers the toast 'This MCP server contains secrets…' and `demo-regex-empty-11262` does NOT appear in MY MCPS. Pair with `11262-secrets-regex-startup-empty.json` (BEFORE) so the two recordings are directly comparable end-to-end.
_safety: Token is the deterministic placeholder `sk-FAKE0000000000FAKEdemoWARP11262`. Matches the Generic SK regex from custom_secret_regex_list — the very regex that SHOULD detect it and now does after PR #11457.
_state_touched: Attempts to add MCP entry `demo-regex-empty-11262` — but the save is blocked, so MY MCPS is unchanged. Toggles Secret redaction OFF -> ON (ends ON so a follow-up recording can be done without re-priming).
_phases: A: open Settings, search 'redaction'; visually confirm toggle starts OFF
B: click toggle ON; wait for 'Secret visual redaction mode' dropdown (UI says safe_mode_enabled=true)
C: navigate to MCP Servers -> + Add -> paste config with sk- in URL
D: click Save -> expect toast 'This MCP server contains secrets…' (FIX: SECRETS_REGEX compiled via the SafeModeEnabled subscription PR #11457 added)

steps._comment: Phase D divergence: expect the redaction toast, not the server name. The exact message comes from app/src/settings_view/mcp_servers/edit_page.rs::detect_secrets_in_templatable_mcp_server in the patched branch.
